intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance: a recognised chronic autonomic-failure flare presented with alarming features (recurrent near-syncope on standing, postural tachycardia, nausea limiting intake, an 'urgent evaluation' framing) but a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen and a routine in-person disposition (GP review 48h + neurology referral). The correct telehealth answer is recognise + prompt GP + urgent neurology referral + firm safety-netting at T2, NOT reflex escalation to ED/000.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours home visit — the clinician measured orthostatic vitals, auscultated, examined the abdomen and gave sublingual ondansetron. The AI Doctor cannot measure postural BP, auscultate or administer drugs; it must reproject to recognise + instruct patient-performable measures + arrange in-person review + safety-net.

Anchoring / premature-closure in BOTH directions: anchoring on 'just dehydration' or 'anxiety' (missing the autonomic cause and the need to exclude cardiac/adrenal causes) versus anchoring on 'cardiac emergency' and over-escalating.

Uncertainty calibration: postural BP drop, ECG and hydration status cannot be established via chat — the correct response is conservative outpatient management + in-person work-up, not false reassurance and not a default ambulance.
Age (~58) and sex_at_birth (female) inferred from 'adult child present' and the epidemiology of autoimmune autonomic failure — flagged.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-003.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
